calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristina Lam appeared by zoom for administrator.

The court asked for an update regarding the reserve. Mrs. Lam stated that $200,000.00 is left, that the administrator is having some issues locating some of the beneficiaries, that one of the beneficiaries is a transient and requested 45 days to locate him. Mrs. Lam also stated that she is not sure if the inheritance tax issue has been resolved, but she will look into it. Status of the reserve is off calendar. The parties can handle dealing with the rest of the reserve on their own.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Continued To Apr-30-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Jun-28-2022 Continued To Oct-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Barbara Simon (administrator) by zoom.

The court stated that its understanding is that it now has the receipts for the distributions, that it now comes down to status of the tax situation, that they need to make it a priority to get the tax issue resolved and asked where things stand. Ms. Simon stated that the income taxes have been filed, that the K-1's have been distributed to the beneficiaries and that because it was a big return the CPA filed a short status which allows the IRS only a year and a half to audit that return. Ms. Simon also stated that the 44-year old inheritance tax return is finished, spoke of the amount of research/reading she had to do regarding the old statutes, stated that there are no instructions, that she has been in contact with the controller's office and that she has finished it/compiled it. In addition, Ms. Simon stated that it was supposed to go out yesterday, but she needed to attach one more form, that it does not appear to her that there is much tax due, that the issue will be whether there is any interest due and that she does not know how long it will take for the controller to process it. Furthermore, Ms. Simon stated that the income taxes were given to the CPA well before the April deadline, that they didn't finish them until May 25th, that the inheritance tax issue is really complicated, that the probate referee wouldn't take it on, that even though she has tax knowledge it was a challenge for her because she had to go back and read all of the legal statutes, and that she did this to the best of her knowledge. Ms. Simon went on to say that she needed to have a number of conversations with the controller, that there was a divorce prior to the decedent's death which complicated the matter because the husband never removed himself from the property, that she is claiming part of a part of a joint tenancy exemption on the return and that she will have to see if the controller will accept that. Discussion was held regarding the importance of filing the return accurately rather than just throwing it together, the tax being due forty years ago, this matter having been open for five years, the title company not catching this, the astute probate examiner having caught this, etc. Ms. Navia stated that because this is so old she doesn't think very many practitioners went into the inheritance problem, that they didn't even think about it, that the biggest thing they were concentrating on is the fact that they have the real property that should have been transferred by the ex-husband, that it didn't get transferred and that they had to deal with the tax issues. Status of the reserve is continued to October 25, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A written status report on the tax issue is to be filed two weeks before the hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Xiao. Not reported

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Continued To Jun-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via Zoom, for and with Barbara Simon, Administrator.

The court, Ms. Navia, and Ms. Simon discuss the possible tax issue with Ms. Simon advising that she has experience with taxes and would require some time to research the tax issue in this matter. The court and Ms. Navia discuss the receipts and whether they've been filed. Ms. Navia believes that they have but the register of actions does not confirm that they have. The court instructs Ms. Navia to file the receipts if they haven't already been filed.

The Status of the Reserve is continued to Jun-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises Ms. Navia to file a brief status report by Jun-21-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Mar-29-2022 Continued To Apr-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-28-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Barbara Simon by zoom.
Thomas Palmer appeared by zoom for David Comer and Nancy Gates (beneficiaries).

The court stated that the third supplemental declaration has not yet been uploaded, but it had a chance to review it and noted that there has been an interesting chain of events. Mr. Palmer stated that he believes he just received the third supplemental declaration, but he has not had a chance to review it. The court stated that it had questions as to whether a reserve of $250,000.00 was enough to cover the inheritance tax, that based on Ms. Simon's declaration it appears to be an ample amount and that it is aware of the tax effect if an order is not signed this year. The court questions why the proposed amount that is to be deducted from Mr. Comer's one third share should not be given to Nancy and Phyllis, and why it should go back to Mr. Comer. Ms. Navia stated that given the fact that it goes back to the estate, it made sense that it be distributed to the three beneficiaries instead of to remainder beneficiaries. Discussion was held regarding the settlement agreement and reference was made to exhibit C of the November 23rd declaration. The court raised the issue of the $2,500.00 that were distributed to David Comer and questions why the checks were payable to Nancy. Ms. Simon explained that David doesn't have a bank account so there was an agreement that it would go to Nancy and then Nancy would give it to David. Ms. Simon also explained that there was a $10,000.00 payment to Legal Assistance to The Elderly on March 20, 2020, that there were four checks at $500.00 to Nancy and that there was another payment of $17,500.00 to Legal Assistance to The Elderly. In addition, Ms. Simon stated that they were trying to give David enough money to move out, that David was supposed to move out in March, but due to the pandemic lockdown he didn't move out until August and that they felt it was right to have the household expenses for that time period allocated to his share. Mr. Palmer stated that he has no objections as to the state of this petition it is in today. The Amended First and Final Account and Report of Adminisrator, etc. is under submission for review of the order with the court's intent to grant the petition. Status of the reserve is set for March 29, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Barbara Simon (Administrator).
Tom Palmer appeared by zoom for David Comer and Nancy Gates.

Ms. Navia requested a 30-day continuance stating that the Decedent died in 1977, that before her death she was involved in a divorce in 1972, that the property was to be transferred by the Husband, that the Husband passed away in 1994, and that her Client is trying to untangle the ownership of the property and determine valuation. Status on close of estate is continued to October 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Continued To May-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roxana Hunt for and with Nancy Gates and for David Comer (Petitioners).
Adam Ferguson for Phyllis Ashen (Petitioner/Respondent).

The Court noted that these are competing petitions, that the Parties have equal priority to serve and asks if the parties have talked as to how they intend to proceed. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss the matter. Upon returning, Mr. Ferguson stated that they have agreed to the appointment of a Private Fiduciary and that they will work out the issue as to who that person will be. The Court informed Counsel that they will need to file an amendment. The Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to May 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an amended petition, or a new petition to appoint a Private Fiduciary.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
